themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over immigrants in access to social services, housing, and healthcare. This theme is evident in Spain, Italy, and France, where parties like Vox, Lega, and Rassemblement National are either implementing or advocating for such measures, often clashing with coalition partners or opposition.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Criticizes PSOE's parking motion in Cangas de Onís as ineffective, proposes concrete parking solutions. Demands Andalusian leader Moreno prioritize locals in policies or face withdrawal of support.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega in Venice removes 'No Mosque' bus ads after complaints, replaces them with boats displaying the same posters. Proposes extending legislative functions to metropolitan cities, not just Rome.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Jordan Bardella vows to remove EU flags from Élysée and Matignon, keeping only the French tricolor if elected president. Supports taxing corporate superprofits amid economic concerns.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS's Czarnek considers working with far-right Confederation, prioritizing unity for Poland's sovereignty. Criticizes government over Zondacrypto scandal and alleged ties to Russian mafia.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK's cover wrap on Abergavenny Chronicle urges voters to 'Vote Reform, Get Labour out,' focusing on immigration control ahead of the May 7 Senedd Election.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Far-right parties are gaining significant traction in polls across Europe, reflecting a broader shift in voter sentiment. This trend is particularly strong in Germany, France, and Sweden, where parties like Alternative für Deutschland, Rassemblement National, and Sverigedemokraterna are either leading or making substantial gains, often at the expense of traditional center-right and center-left parties.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads German polls at 27%, five points ahead of CDU/CSU at 22%. CDU drops to 19% in Berlin poll, with AfD, Greens, and Left tied at 18%.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Marine Le Pen aims for a 2027 runoff against France's centrist bloc, preferring Édouard Philippe over Jean-Luc Mélenchon. RN struggles to secure bank loans for its 2027 presidential campaign.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Sweden Democrats accused of manipulating parliamentary voting system in citizenship rules debate, losing key vote 147-146. Demand urgent meeting over Stockholm's Cosmic healthcare system rollout due to reported care chaos.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK predicted to win most seats on Isle of Wight Council, potentially gaining 26 out of 40 wards. Leads Plaid Cymru 30% to 28% in latest Senedd election poll.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Frp leads polls at 29.1%, with Høyre at 19%, securing a combined majority. Criticizes government's fuel tax cuts pause, citing prioritization of EU rules over domestic businesses.

themes.title: Trump’s policies dominate global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Donald Trump's policies and persona continue to dominate right-wing media coverage worldwide, influencing political discourse and strategies in countries like the UK, Spain, France, and Italy. His administration's actions, from immigration policies to economic measures, are frequently cited as benchmarks or cautionary tales by right-wing parties abroad.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump administration blocks publication of study on COVID vaccine effectiveness. Supreme Court leans toward ending TPS protections for migrants from Haiti and Syria.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK takes out cover wrap on local newspaper vowing to 'control immigration,' mirroring Trump's rhetoric. Nigel Farage faces standards probe over £5m crypto billionaire 'gift' for security.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox criticizes PSOE's motion on parking in Cangas de Onís, proposing concrete parking solutions. Demands Andalusian leader Moreno prioritize locals in policies or face withdrawal of support.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Jordan Bardella supports taxing oil giants' superprofits, as TotalEnergies reports $5.8B Q1 2026 earnings. Vows to replace EU flags with French flags at Élysée and Matignon if elected.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega removes 'No Mosque' bus ads in Venice after Islamic community complaints, replaces them with boats displaying the same posters. Salvini reassures flights will be guaranteed until May amid fuel price tensions.

themes.title: Hungary’s political shift under new leadership
themes.blurb: Hungary's recent election has led to a significant political shift, with Péter Magyar's Tisza Party taking power after 16 years of Viktor Orbán's Fidesz rule. This transition is closely watched by neighboring countries, particularly Poland, and has implications for Hungary's relationship with the EU, including the potential release of frozen funds and changes in domestic policies.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Hungary's Péter Magyar met EU Council President António Costa in Brussels, confirming EU support for unlocking Hungary's funds. Tisza Party plans to abolish the Sovereignty Protection Office, saving 6 billion forints.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS's Czarnek considers working with far-right Confederation, prioritizing unity for Poland's sovereignty. President Nawrocki leads trust rankings with 49% but his advantage narrowed significantly.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads German polls at 27%, five points ahead of CDU/CSU at 22%. Party faces internal power struggles and criticism over ties to extremist groups.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: EU Commission refers Hungary to the Court of Justice over its progressive retail tax, deemed discriminatory against foreign-controlled retail groups. Hungary risks losing billions in EU funds due to corruption concerns.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump administration's policies, such as ending TPS protections for migrants, are closely watched by Hungarian media. Hungary's shift may influence its stance on U.S. policies.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Increasingly inflammatory rhetoric from far-right parties is being linked to a rise in political violence and threats. This theme is evident in Italy, Spain, the UK, and the U.S., where leaders and supporters of far-right movements face backlash, protests, and even physical attacks, highlighting the growing polarization in these societies.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: AI-generated video falsely claimed Italian PM Giorgia Meloni ignored Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu at a 2026 UNGA session. Lega removes 'No Mosque' bus ads in Venice after Islamic community complaints.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox's distribution of party bracelets outside Almassora's IES Vila-Roja sparked police intervention due to tensions. Criticized for 'No Mosque' campaign and anti-immigration rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Nigel Farage reported to watchdog over £5m crypto billionaire 'gift' for security after firebomb attack. Reform UK candidate faces questions over posting abuse of PM.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump administration disbands NSF governing board, raising concerns over political interference. James Comey appears in court over alleged threat against President Trump.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Jordan Bardella vows to remove EU flags from Élysée and Matignon if elected president. Hundreds protest in Carcassonne against far-right mayor Christophe Barthès and his policies.
